content.full_text:
Fnrn Polrrrcal PnecrrcEs CouMrssroN
428 J Street. Suite 620 . Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660. Fax (916) 322-0886
October 4,2002
Mr. David Bauer
400 capitol Mall, suite 1560
Sacramento, CA95814
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. A-02-259
Dear Mr. Bauer:
This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of Irvine
Homeowners Association regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the "Act").1
QUESTIONS
l. Other than major donor filing requirements, if applicable, are there additional
filing obligations for one who contributes to the Irvine Homeowners Association, a
committee that receives contributions for the purpose of making independent
expenditures and which has identified several candidates who will be the targets of
independent expenditures.
2. May the Irvine Homeowners Association accept contributions from a
candidate's controlled committee? May a candidate's controlled committee make such a
contribution?
CONCLUSIONS
1. Under the facts you have described, a contributor to the Irvine Homeowners
Association will be fully disclosed on the committee's periodic reports and does not incur
any additional filing obligations with respect to independent expenditures made by the
committee. The contributor is, of course, required to file disclosure reports as a major
donor if the contributor meets the $10,000 per year threshold that triggers major donor
filing.
I Government Code sections 81000 - gl}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
t:


File No. A-02-259
Page No. 2
2. A candidate's controlled committee may not make contributions to the Irvine
Homeowners Association, a committee that receives contributions for the purpose of
rnaking independent expenditures. Section 85501 provides that "[a] controlled
committee of a candidatemay not make independent expenditures and may not contribute
funds to another committee for the purpose of making independent expenditures to
support or oppose other candidates."
FACTS
You are the treasurer for the Irvine Homeowners Association, a recipient
committee whose purpose is making independent expenditures. The committee will be
active in city elections and may possibly have some involvement in a county or a state
race. The committee solicits contributions by identifring to potential contributors those
candidates who will be the targets of independent expenditures made by the committee.
Contributions will be made to the committee with the knowledge of which candidates are
targeted, but without specific knowledge of the date, type and value of the independent
expenditures. Less than one hundred percent of the funds raised will be spent on
independent expenditures, with some portion used for administrative expenses and a
surplus carried over to subsequent election cycles.
ANALYSIS
L. Contributor's filing obligations. Your first question asks whether
contributors to the Irvine Homeowners Association have any direct report filing
obligations with respect to independent expendifures the committee makes, in addition to
the usual filing obligations the contributors incur if they are a major donor to California
elections.
According to your facts, the Irvine Homeowners Association has solicited
contributions by identifring to potential contributors those candidates who will be the
target of independent expenditures made by the committee. Though the contributors
know which candidates will be targeted by the committee, when the contributors are
making donations to the committee they are not funding specific expenditures or
mailings. As stated above, contributors who are making donations to the Irvine
Homeowners Association do not have any specific knowledge of the date, type or value
of independent expenditures that the committee will make. in addition, ,o-" portion of
the contributions will be used for the committee's administrative expenses and a surplus
may be carried over to future elections. You want to make sure such contributions to an
independent expenditure committee would not be considered 'oearmarked" under the Act,
or require additional disclosure by the contributors.
With respect to earmarking, section 85704 provides as follows
"A person may not make any contribution to a committee on the
condition or with the agreement that it will be contributed to any particular


File No. A-02-259
Page No. 3
candidate unless the contribution is fully disclosed pursuant to Section
84302;',
Section 84302 sets forth the Act's disclosure requirements for contributions made
through an intermediary or an agent. Section 84302 states:
"No person shall make a contribution on behalf of another, or while
actingas the intermediary or agent of another, without disclosing to the
recipient of the contribution both his own fulI name and street address,
occupation, and the name of his employer, if any, or his principal place of
business if he is self-employed, and the full name and street address,
occupation, and the name of employer, if any, or principal place of
business if self-employed, of the other person. The recipient of the
contribution shall include in his campaign statement the full name and
street address, occupation, and the name of the employer, if any, or the
principal place of business if self-employed, of both the intermediary and
the contributor."
In the situation you have described, however, the Act's earmarking and
intermediary statutes do not apply. Contributors to the Irvine Homeowners Association
will be fully disclosed on the committee's periodic reports. The Irvine Homeowners
Association is a recipient committee that makes independent expenditures. (Section
82013(a).) It will file reports as a recipient committee, including semi-annual disclosure
reports, pre-election reports, late reports, and supplemental independent expenditure
reports (ifnecessary).
Under the facts you have described, contributors to the Irvine Homeowners
Association do not incur any additional direct filing responsibilities in connection with
independent expenditures made by the Irvine Homeowners Association. A contributor to
the Irvine Homeowners Association has independent filing obligations as a major donor,
of course, if the contributor makes contributions totaling $10,000 or more in a calendar
year to Califomia candidates or committees.
2. Prohibition on candidate controlled committees making independent
expenditures. Proposition34 added section 85501 to the Act which prohibits a
controlled committee of a candidate from making independent expenditures. Section
85501 provides as follows:
"A controlled committee of a candidate may not make independent
expenditures and may not contribute funds to another committee for the
purpose of making independent expenditures to support or oppose other
candidates."
Section 85501 is applicable to both state and local candidates and committees.
Accordingly, a candidate's controlled committee may not make a contribution to the


File No. A-02-259
Page No. 4
Irvine Homeowners Association, a committee whose purpose is to make independent
expenditures.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
6${" {- day'*z
By: Hyla P. Wagner
Counsel, Legal Divi sion
HPwjg

t:

rnaking independent expenditures. Section 85501 provides that "[a] controlled
committee of a candidatemay not make independent expenditures and may not contribute

support or oppose other candidates."
